title: Tuning Tips for Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
Tuning Tips for Microsoft SQL Server
text: Tuning Tips for Microsoft SQL Server The Microsoft SQL Server can be tuned to improve performance with a Uniface application. SQL Profiler You can monitor the performance of DBMS events corresponding to Uniface operations using SQL Profiler. SQL Profiler is a Microsoft SQL server tool that can help to detect the database problem and also help to optimize the SQL server database. Use the following setting in the assignment file of the Uniface Application Server: [DRIVER_SETTINGS] mss=U3.0 USYS$MSS_PARAMS mapping:2,procs:on,ri:dbms Copy For every database I/O operation ( retrieve , store , update , delete , and insert ) performed on the Uniface client application, note the corresponding stored procedures or SQL statement executed on the SQL server. The performance problems are associated with the time it takes to execute insert , update and select statements. To analyze performance: Start the SQL Profiler and start a trace—Choose File >New >Trace… on the SQL Server. To capture the triggers or stored procedures, set the SP:StmtCompleted options—Choose Trace Properties >Events tab >Stored Procedures to choose the events you want to monitor. View the trace data. The TextData trace data column identifies the trigger or stored procedure names from the TextData column. This also displays the code found within the trigger or stored procedure itself. You can copy and paste the trigger code into Query Analyzer for further troubleshooting and query analysis. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
